content: re86 L1 solved in 48 steps. L2 in progress — 3 pieces (orange X, red diamond, blue cross), single 64x64 target sprite with colors 9/12/13. Target centers: red at (20,8), orange at (14,46), blue at (25,48). Need compute exact moves per piece from source positions. L2 budget 100 steps.

L2 GRID ALIGNMENT: pieces start on different mod-3 grids than targets. Blue at mod(2,2), orange at mod(1,1), targets at mod(0,0). Movement is 3px steps = same mod forever. MUST use wall collision (border-shifting for normal pieces, deformation for nogegkgqgd) to shift pieces to correct grid alignment. This is a CORE MECHANIC for all levels beyond L1. The red piece (nogegkgqgd = deformable) changes shape on wall collision. Normal pieces shift borders. Both result in position changes not on the 3-grid.

re86 L1-L2 solved. L3: 3 pieces ALL color 8 (red), different shapes (1x43 line, 25x25 X, 23x23 diamond). Individual pieces dont match target subsets — need COMPOSITE matching. All 3 pieces overlaid on canvas must match target. This is a shape-composition puzzle: find (x,y) for each piece so combined rendering matches target pattern. No walls, no paint zones. One piece is deformable (nogegkgqgd). 200-step budget.

L4: Pink recoloring to orange WORKS (27-frame animation at step 12). Cyan recolor to green also works (21 frames). But final positioning is wrong — target offset computation needs to account for recolored piece shape and center pixel behavior. The center pixel changes after recolor (active marker). Need to verify target positions with actual rendered pixels after recoloring.

paint_zones.spreading_animation: zone touch triggers flood-fill that paints ENTIRE piece to zone color over multiple animation frames in step(). perform_action handles all frames automatically — 1 step cost regardless of animation duration.
paint_zones.zone_iteration: zones checked in sprite list order. FIRST zone where piece color differs triggers. Function returns immediately — later zones never checked.
paint_zones.pixel_level: initial contact only paints overlapping pixels, but spreading animation then converts ALL non-(-1) pixels to zone color

wall_deformation.dx_collision: width-3, height+3. LEFT into wall: position undone. RIGHT into wall: position stays.
wall_deformation.dy_collision: height-3, width+3. UP into wall: position undone. DOWN into wall: position stays.
wall_deformation.min_check: checks width/height > 6 BEFORE deforming. So 7→4 IS possible (check passes at 7, result is 4).
wall_deformation.color_preserved: deformation creates new pixel array with pbqnrradrp (piece color). Color survives deformation.

border_shifting.mechanism: wall collision shifts arm col or row by ±3 depending on direction and which arm overlaps
border_shifting.dx_jigozizhda_only: arm shifts toward collision, position stays
border_shifting.dy_xlozfjsllh_only: arm shifts toward collision, position stays

data.piece_collision: pieces do NOT collide with each other. Only miqpqafylc (wall) sprites block movement. Pieces freely overlap.
data.win_check: composites ALL vfaeucgcyr piece pixels onto pseflysmdl canvas. Checks vzuwsebntu target pixels. Color 4 = wildcard (any non-(-1) value OK).

data.barrier: 9 paint zones at rows 28-32 span cols -3..61 with gaps of max 6 cols (at 44-49). Paint spreading makes ANY zone touch repaint entire piece.
data.required_colors: c6 (zone at rows 20-24) and c11 (zone at rows 5-9) — both ABOVE barrier. Targets at y=39-57 — BELOW barrier.
data.deform_to_4x22: 3 dx deforms: 13x13→10x16→7x19→4x22. Width 4 fits in 6-col gap at cols 44-49.
data.blocking_zones_above: c9 at (28,8) rows 8-12, c12 at (28,21) rows 21-25 — block horizontal transit between c6 zone (cols 5-9) and barrier gap (cols 44-49)
data.approach: deform to 4x22, navigate through gap, paint from above, return through gap. Complex routing needed to avoid c9 and c12 zones during horizontal transit.

data.l8_key_insight: 22x4 shape (via dy-deform chain) is only 4 rows tall, fitting in y=15 safe corridor between c9 zone (rows 8-12) and c12 zone (rows 21-25). This enables free horizontal transit across entire screen width between the c6/c11 paint zones and the barrier gap.
data.l8_route: dy-deform at wall (34,55) below barrier, cross barrier UP, dy-deform 2x at wall (58,1) above barrier to 22x4, paint at zone, return via safe corridor, dx-deform 6x at wall (58,1) to 4x22 (4 cols wide), cross barrier DOWN through gap (cols 44-49), reshape at wall (34,55), position at target.
